Vogtland (German:[ˈfoːktlant] ⓘ; [1][2] Czech: Fojtsko) is a region spanning the German states of Bavaria, Saxony and Thuringia and north-western Bohemia in the Czech Republic. It overlaps with and is largely contained within Euroregio Egrensis. A gently rising plateau with a northern region of several deep river valleys, Germany’s Vogtland has broad meadows, forested hills, and medium-high mountains (known as the Upper Vogtland) to the southeast. Vogtland is a region in southwestern Saxony, bordering Thuringia, Upper Franconia and West Bohemia (Czech Republic). It is characterised by idyllic rolling hills cut through by river valleys and known for its handicraft traditions like lace-making and musical instrument making. Vogtland is a region in southwestern Saxony, bordering Thuringia, Upper Franconia and West Bohemia (Czech Republic). It is characterised by idyllic rolling hills cut through by river valleys and known for its handicraft traditions like lace-making and musical instrument making. Vogtland is a region spanning the German states of Bavaria, Saxony and Thuringia and north-western Bohemia in the Czech Republic. It overlaps with and is largely contained within Euroregio Egrensis. The name alludes to the former leadership by the Vögte of Weida, Gera and Plauen. Vogtland is a region in southwestern Saxony, bordering Thuringia, Upper Franconia and West Bohemia. It is characterised by idyllic rolling hills cut through by river valleys and known for its handicraft traditions like lace-making and musical instrument making. Plauen is the administrative centre and largest city of the district. Other major cities (Große Kreisstädte) are Reichenbach im Vogtland, Auerbach, and Oelsnitz im Vogtland. The Vogtland became part of the Holy Roman Empire under king Conrad III in the 12th century. Neben tollen Outdooraktivitäten wie Wandern, Radfahren, Skifahren, Langlauf oder Reiten kann man im Vogtland natürlich auch wunderbare Tagesausflüge zu Museen, Bauwerken, Bergwerken, Talsperren und vieles andere mehr unternehmen. It is characterised by idyllic rolling hills cut through by river valleys and known for its handicraft traditions like lace-making and musical instrument making. Vogtland, physical and cultural region of southwestern Saxony Land (state), southeastern Germany, lying between Bavaria Land and the Czech Republic. Das Vogtland ist ein Teil der Euregio Egrensis, eines Vereins mit Arbeitsgemeinschaften in Bayern, Sachsen, Thüringen und Tschechien. Das Vogtland gilt als eine der vulkanisch aktivsten Zonen in Mitteleuropa. Anzeichen dafür sind Schwarmbeben, Thermalquellen und Gasaustritte (Mofetten). Vogtland is a region spanning the German states of Bavaria, Saxony and Thuringia and north-western Bohemia in the Czech Republic. It overlaps with and is largely contained within Euroregio Egrensis. The name alludes to the former leadership by the Vögte of Weida, Gera and Plauen. Romantic landscapes and seemingly endless alpine forests are particularly captivating when snow transforms the Vogtland into a winter wonderland. That’s when the Kammloipe – one of Germany’s finest cross-country ski runs – calls for an excursion to the ski fields. Welcome to Saxony and Thuringia - yes, welcome to the Vogtland! Our region offers a multifaceted experience for families, hikers, culture explorers and nature lovers at any time of year. Here you can experience unique activities and fascinating sights that captivate every visitor. Vogtlandkreis is famous for its industrial communities and idyllic mountain landscape. Crystal clear mountain springs, densely forested hillsides and snow-covered peaks await you. Willkommen in Sachsen und in Thüringen - Ja, willkommen im Vogtland!
